title: muxcover pass stalls if $_MUX_ output is disconnected
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.50+14 (git sha1 eb5abf9c1, ccache clang++ 18.1.8 -fPIC -O3)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Not sure how likely this is to come up in the wild, but while investigating #4590 (and its possible connection to #4722) I discovered that if muxcover gets called with a `$_MUX_` cell where the output (`Y`), and at least two inputs (`A`, `B`, `S`) are disconnected (set to `1'x`) then `muxcover` gets stuck in an infinite loop.
e.g. stall_muxcover.il
```
module \top

  wire input 1 \a
  wire input 2 \b
  wire input 3 \s
  wire output 4 \y

  cell $_MUX_ \badmux
    connect \A 1'x
    connect \B 1'x
    connect \S \s
    connect \Y 1'x
  end
end
```
and calling
`yosys -p muxcover stall_muxcover.il`

### Expected Behavior

No infinite loop, maybe a log error, at the very least an assertion error.

### Actual Behavior

```
-- Parsing `temp_tests/stall_muxcover.il' using frontend `rtlil' --

1. Executing RTLIL frontend.
Input filename: temp_tests/stall_muxcover.il

-- Running command `muxcover' --

2. Executing MUXCOVER pass (mapping to wider MUXes).
Covering MUX trees in module top..
  Treeifying 1 MUXes:
^C
```
code_blocks:
module \top

  wire input 1 \a
  wire input 2 \b
  wire input 3 \s
  wire output 4 \y

  cell $_MUX_ \badmux
    connect \A 1'x
    connect \B 1'x
    connect \S \s
    connect \Y 1'x
  end
end
